phase1.output: (Window appears with GridPane form and BorderPane layout)
phase1.explanation:
GridPane and BorderPane are powerful layout managers:

GridPane:
• Arranges nodes in a grid (rows and columns)
• add(node, column, row) — adds node at position
• setHgap(double) — horizontal gap between columns
• setVgap(double) — vertical gap between rows
• setRowSpan() / setColumnSpan() — spans multiple rows/columns
• Great for forms and calculators

BorderPane:
• Divides into five regions: Top, Bottom, Left, Center, Right
• setTop(), setBottom(), setLeft(), setCenter(), setRight()
• Center grows to fill remaining space
• Great for main application layouts

Row/Column constraints:
• GridPane.setRowIndex(node, row)
• GridPane.setColumnIndex(node, column)
• GridPane.setColumnSpan(node, span)

Halignment/Valignment:
• GridPane.setHalignment(node, HPos.CENTER)
• GridPane.setValignment(node, VPos.CENTER)

When to use:
• GridPane: forms, calculators, tabular data
• BorderPane: main application layout with menu/status bar

phase2.errorMessage: (Window appears — but both nodes are in row 0, column 0, overlapping!)

phase3.prompt:
Write a JavaFX program that:
1. Creates a BorderPane
2. Sets Top to a Label "My Dashboard"
3. Sets Center to a GridPane with:
   - Row 0: "Product" and "Price" labels
   - Row 1: "Laptop" and "$1200" labels
   - Row 2: "Phone" and "$800" labels
4. Sets Bottom to an HBox with Buttons: "Add", "Delete", "Update"
5. Shows the window
